channels.title: Channel Management
channels.name: Channel Name
channels.baseUrlSuffixTip: ⚠️ The system appends /v1/chat/completions automatically. Do NOT add /v1 suffix
channels.weightTip: 💡 Channel priority within the same group. Higher value = higher priority. This is channel-level ranking (separate from account priority)
channels.maxRPMTip: 💡 Max requests per minute per account in this channel. 0 = unlimited
channels.maxTPMTip: 💡 Max tokens per minute per account in this channel. 0 = unlimited
channels.maxDailyRequestsTip: 💡 Max requests per day per account in this channel. Account will be skipped once limit is reached. 0 = unlimited
channels.testKeyPlaceholder: Test key (leave empty to use channel account)
channels.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter API Key for this channel
channels.noModelsTip: Please add an account first to fetch model list
channels.selectOwner: Select a provider on the left
channels.mappingInvalidContent: The following mapped models are not selected: {names}
channels.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete channel "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
channels.noModelsConfigured: No models configured yet, please fetch and configure models first
channels.noBaseUrl: Base URL not configured, please set it first
channels.noActiveAccount: No active account available, please add and enable an account
channels.searchPlaceholder: Search name, ID, type or model...
channels.testModelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use first configured model
channels.testModelHint: Model used for availability test. Leave empty to auto-select the first configured model
channels.channelCopied: Channel copied, please configure accounts for the new channel
channels.noRemovedModels: All models are online, no deprecated models found

groups.selectGroupHint: Click a group name from the left panel to edit
groups.name: Group Name
groups.channelWeightHint: Determines priority when competing with other groups. Higher value = higher priority
groups.channelPriorityTip: Channel priority is determined by its own weight (higher = more preferred). Channels within a group are tried in descending weight order, with lower-weight channels used as fallbacks when higher-weight channels are unavailable.
groups.quotaRPMHint: Max requests per minute for each key in this group. 0 = unlimited
groups.quotaTPMHint: Max tokens per minute for each key in this group. 0 = unlimited
groups.deleteBlocked: This group is currently in use and cannot be deleted

plugins.title: Plugin Manager
plugins.name: Plugin Name
plugins.author: Author
plugins.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall this plugin? This cannot be undone!
plugins.emptyDesc: Click upload to install a plugin
plugins.channelConfigExists: Config already exists for this channel
plugins.registryEmpty: No plugins available in the registry
plugins.registryNotConfigured: Plugin registry URL is not configured. Please set plugin_registry_url in System Settings
plugins.permEmpty: This plugin has no permission declarations
plugins.permHighSensitiveMsg: Permission '{name}' is high sensitive. It may allow the plugin to access request headers (including auth info) or full channel config (including proxy addresses). Confirm grant?

settings.title: System Settings
settings.serverDesc: Application run mode and debug settings
settings.logDesc: Log level, storage path and retention policy
settings.proxyDesc: Upstream API connection and timeout settings
settings.accountManagerDesc: Account health probe, failure cooldown and cache policies
settings.serverModeHint: Debug mode logs everything; release mode logs errors and warnings only
settings.logLevelHint: debug < info < warn < error. Use "info" for production
settings.logDirHint: Directory for log files, relative to the working directory
settings.logMaxAgeHint: Log files older than this will be auto-cleaned. 0 disables cleaning. Recommended: 7-90 days
settings.detailLogEnabledHint: When enabled, full request/response content is saved as individual JSON files after each successful request. When disabled, only summary stats are kept
settings.connectTimeoutHint: Timeout for establishing connections to upstream APIs. Recommended: 3-10s
settings.readTimeoutHint: Timeout for waiting upstream responses. For long/streaming: 120-300s
settings.maxIdleConnsHint: HTTP connection pool size. For high concurrency: 100-1000
settings.idleConnTimeoutHint: Max lifetime of idle connections in the pool. Recommended: 60-120s
settings.streamReadTimeoutHint: Max wait between stream chunks. 0=unlimited. Cold-start channels may need 300s+. Recommended: 120-600s
settings.affinityTTLHint: Sticky binding duration per consumer-channel pair. Too long causes load imbalance
settings.consecutiveFailureThresholdHint: Auto-disable an account after consecutive request failures reach this count. Recommended: 3-10
settings.minDisableDurationHint: Minimum wait time after an account is disabled, during which it won't be probed. Recommended: 60-300s
settings.probeIntervalHint: On-demand probe trigger interval. Probes start when active ratio falls below threshold. Recommended: 30-120s
settings.probeActiveRatioThresholdHint: Trigger probe when active account ratio in a channel falls below this (0.3-0.5)
settings.maxProbeFailuresHint: Stop probing an account after its consecutive probe failures reach this count. Recommended: 5-20
settings.maxProbeRecoverPerCycleHint: Max accounts to recover per probe cycle, preventing mass recovery and repeated failures
settings.probeCooldownDurationHint: First-level cooldown after an account is disabled. No probe recovery during cooldown. Recommended: 3600-7200s (1-2h)
settings.probeCooldownDurationL2Hint: Second-level cooldown for repeatedly disabled accounts. Longer than L1 to prevent flapping. Recommended: 21600-86400s (6-24h)
settings.globalHealthCheckIntervalHint: Interval for health patrol across all disabled accounts in all channels. Recommended: 1800-7200s
settings.accountStatusCacheTTLHint: In-memory cache duration for account active status, used for quick affinity validation. Recommended: 30-120s
settings.accountKeyCacheTTLHint: In-memory cache duration for decrypted API keys, avoiding repeated decryption. Recommended: 60-300s

login.title: AIGateway Admin Login
login.failed: Login failed, please check your username and password

models.title: Model Management
models.visibilityOn: This model will be returned in /v1/models
models.noModelsHint: Please configure model mappings in channels first. Models will be synced here automatically.

monitor.title: Monitor & Alerts
monitor.testIntervalHint: Automatically probe all channels in the background
monitor.disableOnFailureHint: Accumulate consecutive failures on test failure, auto-disable when threshold reached
monitor.maxRetryAttemptsHint: Skip channel after N cross-account failures in same request
monitor.failureExcludeKeywordsHint: Errors containing these keywords won't count as failures (e.g., context canceled)
monitor.disableStatusCodes: Status codes to immediately disable account
monitor.latencyThresholdHint: Auto-disable channels exceeding this response time (0=no limit)
monitor.disableStatusCodesHint: Immediately disable account when these status codes are matched
monitor.retryStatusCodesHint: Trigger account-level retry when these status codes are matched
monitor.failureKeywordsHint: If upstream error contains any of these keywords/phrases (case-insensitive), account will be auto-disabled
monitor.keywordsPlaceholder: Enter keyword or phrase, press Enter to add

hero.subtitle: High-Performance AI API Aggregation Gateway
hero.description: Unified management of multiple AI service providers' API keys, channels, and accounts. Smart routing, automatic failover, and usage statistics for reliable AI API calls.

multi.title: Multi-Protocol
multi.desc: Native support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini and more. Automatic request format conversion.

route.title: Smart Routing
route.desc: Hierarchical deterministic routing engine with key groups, channel groups, model filters and priority scheduling.

plugin.title: Plugin System
plugin.desc: Sidecar proxy for TLS fingerprinting, request rewriting and more. Non-invasive extensibility.

keys.title: Key Management
keys.desc: AES-256-GCM encrypted API key storage with RPM/TPM quota control and usage alerts.

stats.title: Monitoring
stats.desc: Real-time request logs, daily aggregated stats, dashboard visualization for full API observability.

ha.title: High Availability
ha.desc: Automatic fault detection and recovery, account-level cooldown and retry for continuous service.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.comingSoon: Documentation is under development. Stay tuned!

about.description: A high-performance, multi-tenant AI API aggregation gateway. Unified multi-provider key management, intelligent routing, automatic failover, and full-chain monitoring.
